rulings.Case Title: VAUGHN, LEE, AS SUCCESSOR-IN-INTEREST TO AND AS et al VS. PARKER-HANNIFIN CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication

rulings.Case Title: WAYNE RUNNESTRAND et al VS. HONEYWELL INTERNATIONAL, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Henkel Corporation To Seal Documents Lodged In Support Of Its Summary Judgment Motion
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, June 9, 2016 in Department 503, Line 2. Defendant Henkel Corporation's motion to seal documents lodged in support of its summary judgment motion is granted. Defendant must submit a proposed order in compliance with CRC 2.550(d), (e) and 2.551(e). The order will apply to the following documents in support of defendant's motion for summary judgment dated May 4, 2016: Exhibits A and B to the declaration of James Martin, attached as Exhibit 19 to the declaration of Jessica Fakhimi in support of defendant's MSJ; the declaration of John Spencer and Exhibit B thereto, attached as Exhibit 15 to the declaration of Jessica Fakhimi in support of defendant's MSJ. The order will also apply to the following documents in support of defendant's motion to seal dated May 4, 2016: Exhibits 1 and 2 to the declaration of Karen St. Onge in support of defendant's motion to seal. No opposition filed. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/GLW)
